    <item>
      <title>Unlikely hero Luke Kennard leads Lakers to Game 1 win over Rockets</title>
      <description>With Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves both out of action and unlikely to return in the series, the Los Angeles Lakers were led by an unlikely hero on Saturday night. </description>
      <pubDate>18 Apr 2026 23:22:58 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/unlikely_hero_luke_kennard_leads_lakers_to_game_1_win_over_rockets/s1_13132_43745036</link>
      <guid>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/unlikely_hero_luke_kennard_leads_lakers_to_game_1_win_over_rockets/s1_13132_43745036</guid>
      <author>Mike J. Asti, Yardbarker</author>
      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/e/d/edfcbdfde2d485ca5685ba9c1c539693e0741793/thumb_16x9/unlikely-hero-luke-kennard-leads-lakers-game-1-win.jpg">
        <media:credit> Kirby Lee-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Los Angeles Lakers guard Luke Kennard (10) celebrates against the Houston Rockets in the second half during Game 1 of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Crypto.com Arena. </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Los Angeles Lakers</category>
      <category>Houston Rockets</category>

      <category>LeBron James</category>

    <item>
      <title>Five moves the Hornets need to make to take the next step</title>
      <description>The Hornets were a pleasant surprise this season despite falling short in the play-in tournament. With a solid young core, here are five move the team needs to make to take the next step.</description>
      <pubDate>19 Apr 2026 09:16:42 -0400</pubDate>

    </item>
    <item>
      <title>Five moves Warriors need to make to return to glory</title>
      <description>With a roster full of players on the wrong side of 30, two players recovering from serious knee injuries and multiple expiring contracts, the Warriors are at another transition point.</description>

    <item>
      <title>All-Star duo leads Nuggets to ugly Game 1 win over Timberwolves</title>
      <description>The Denver Nuggets couldn't make threes, lost Aaron Gordon to foul trouble and fell behind by double digits early. Thanks to patience, defense and their All-Star duo, Denver took Game One over the Minnesota Timberwolves anyway.</description>
      <pubDate>18 Apr 2026 19:16:31 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/all_star_duo_leads_nuggets_to_ugly_game_1_win_over_timberwolves/s1_13132_43744454</link>

      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/6/a/6a3b9fb18e21f0f89d641f5aa18111c4bdf2fd0b/thumb_16x9/star-duo-leads-nuggets-ugly-game-1-win-over.jpg">
        <media:credit> Christopher Hanewinckel-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Denver Nuggets center Nikola Jokic (15) is fouled by Minnesota Timberwolves guard Anthony Edwards (5) as he handles the ball during the first half in game one of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Ball Arena.  </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Denver Nuggets</category>
      <category>Minnesota Timberwolves</category>
      <category>Nikola Jokic</category>
      <category>Aaron Gordon</category>
      <category>Jamal Murray</category>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>Nikola Jokic, Jamal Murray show why Nuggets are still NBA title contenders</title>

      <title>Red-hot Jalen Green dazzles in stunning Warriors takedown as Suns claim West's last playoff spot</title>

    <item>
      <title>Draymond Green gets ejected in what could be final game with Warriors</title>
      <description>If Friday was Draymond Green's final game with the Golden State Warriors, he went out in classic Draymond fashion.</description>
